description: PUH-BrianMartell-T223 resolves this cleanly: the two values are correct in their respective bases. The difference is exactly the CKM 23-mixing angle theta_23 = 1/27 rad (≈ 2.12°). •  In the Koide-diagonal basis (natural for sector-by-sector mass structure and Z₃ phases from T152), each sector is diagonalized independently. CKM mixing appears as a rotation between up and down planes. •  In the weak basis (used by Zenczykowski), the up-type is the reference frame (diagonal). The CKM rotation needed to diagonalize the down-type is absorbed into the apparent down-type phase offset. The translation rule is simple: delta_dn_weak = delta_dn_diag − theta_23 Plugging in your values: −3/27 − 1/27 = −4/27 → exactly matches Zenczykowski.
